label: startup error
evidenceLines: [Server thread/ERROR]: Unable to start Minecraft server
safeFirstStep: Stop the server process, archive the current logs, and confirm the latest jar is intact.
likelyCause: Corrupted Paper jar or missing JVM dependency after manual download.
customerMessage: Startup failed because one of the server binaries is missing or unreadable; I'll verify the jar and JVM before resuming.
resolutionNotes: Replaced jar with a known-good download, verified checksum, and server boots cleanly.

label: plugin/config conflict
evidenceLines: [Server thread/ERROR]: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Cannot set property 'world-edit' to 'true'
safeFirstStep: Stop accepting player connections, snapshot `plugins` and `config` directories, and disable the suspect plugin.
likelyCause: New WorldGuard config option contradicts Paper protection defaults.
customerMessage: A recent config change is incompatible with Paper's protection defaults; I'm reverting the setting and verifying.
evidenceToCollect: Full stack trace around the plugin load
resolutionNotes: Pushed patched config via git; plugin loads after reapplying older option semantics.

label: version mismatch
evidenceLines: [Async Chat Thread]: Connection refused: target version 1.16.5 while server is 1.20
safeFirstStep: Stop the bridge service, note the server/proxy versions, and back up current configs.
likelyCause: Proxy is still running 1.16 client handshake against Paper 1.20.
customerMessage: The bridge is speaking the wrong protocol version; I'm stopping it and applying the matching build.
resolutionNotes: Rolled back proxy to 1.20-compatible build and restarted; connections succeeded.

label: likely infrastructure issue
evidenceLines: [Server thread/WARN]: Failed to bind to port 25565: Address already in use
safeFirstStep: Disable traffic by removing the container from the load balancer, then inspect host-level sockets.
likelyCause: Legacy process (maybe previous Paper container) still bound to 25565 on the host.
customerMessage: The host is already using port 25565, so the server can't bind; I'm identifying the conflicting service before restarting.
evidenceToCollect: Docker ps/logs for any other Paper container
resolutionNotes: Stopped stale Paper container, freed port, and updated Compose to use a dedicated bridge network for clarity.

label: startup error
evidenceLines: [init script] Starting server with NO_FILE permission
safeFirstStep: Stop the systemd unit, verify file permissions, and back up the jar directory.
likelyCause: The paper.jar file lost execute/read rights after a sudo chown.
customerMessage: The jar file's permissions changed, so the JVM can't run it yet; I'll restore ownership and verify safe start.
resolutionNotes: Corrected file ownership, then started the service under the configured user.

label: plugin/config conflict
safeFirstStep: Stop the server, snapshot the plugin jar versions, and check plugin manager compatibility notes.
likelyCause: AuthMe jar is built against Bukkit API but current Paper removed the class.
customerMessage: AuthMe is built for a different API release; I'm matching the plugin build to this server version before restarting.
resolutionNotes: Swapped in plugin version compatible with current Paper; server loads cleanly.

label: version mismatch
evidenceLines: [Async Chat Thread]: SQLState 08S01: Communication link failure
safeFirstStep: Stop the bridge service, take a config backup, and document driver versions.
likelyCause: Old JDBC driver linking to new MySQL server.
customerMessage: The connector library is incompatible with the database; I'm collecting evidence and will deploy the supported driver.
resolutionNotes: Deployed JDBC 8.0.33, restarted service, and connections stabilized.

label: likely infrastructure issue
safeFirstStep: Drain traffic, capture `iptables -L`, and recreate the routing table before touching configs.
likelyCause: Load balancer routing missing after a recent Docker network restart.
customerMessage: The load balancer isn't reaching the container; I'm checking routes and will rebuild the service safely.
resolutionNotes: Reloaded Docker net and LB; restored connectivity without changing server settings.

label: likely infrastructure issue
evidenceLines: [Server thread/ERROR]: OOM killer invoked, killed process 14321 (java)
dmesg: Out of memory: Kill process 14321
safeFirstStep: Stop accepting workloads, take a snapshot, and send memory stats to platform team.
likelyCause: Host is under memory pressure from another container, not the Minecraft service itself.
customerMessage: The host ran out of memory and killed the JVM; I'm escalating to infrastructure to free RAM before we start again.
resolutionNotes: Infrastructure freed RAM and adjusted limits; node stabilized.

label: plugin/config conflict
evidenceLines: Dynmap reported chunk coordinate 0,0 out of bounds
safeFirstStep: Stop the server, take a config snapshot, and clear Dynmap caches.
likelyCause: Dynmap cache folder corrupted after a manual file signature update.
customerMessage: Dynmap's cache is corrupted; I'll clear the cache and restart once the server is stopped.
resolutionNotes: Deleted cache and let Dynmap rebuild after restart; map renders normally.

label: version mismatch
evidenceLines: [ApiWorker/ERROR]: Client expects schema v4, gateway serves v5
safeFirstStep: Stop routing new requests, back up API configs, and document schema changes.
likelyCause: Rolling deploy introduced breaking schema change without client coordination.
customerMessage: The API contract changed unexpectedly; I'm rolling back to v4 while coordinating on an upgrade plan.
resolutionNotes: Rolled gateway back to v4, notified product, and scheduled controlled migration.

label: likely infrastructure issue
safeFirstStep: Pause writes, snapshot the disk, and collect SMART health before making changes.
likelyCause: Underlying storage is saturated or failing; not the Minecraft process itself.
customerMessage: Host disk is saturated; I'm collecting evidence and engaging infrastructure to validate the array.
resolutionNotes: Infra swapped to new volume; everything moved over without downtime.

label: startup error
safeFirstStep: Ensure no active players, save server state, and capture start parameters.
likelyCause: Start script launched in offline mode purposely; verifying that's expected.
customerMessage: The server started in offline mode as configured; I'm double-checking the property to confirm it was intentional.
resolutionNotes: Documented the offline mode requirement for the customer and flagged it in runbook.

label: needs escalation
evidenceLines: Multiple container logs show clean shutdown simultaneously
safeFirstStep: Confirm generator status, isolate power-sensitive work, and notify infrastructure lead.
likelyCause: Power flicker triggered emergency shutdown; requires facility support.
customerMessage: The data center lost power and the servers went offline; I'm elevating to facilities to restore stable power.
resolutionNotes: Facility team replaced failing UPS; restarted containers once power stabilized.

label: needs escalation
safeFirstStep: Stop backup cron, preserve existing snapshots locally, and collect provider status.
likelyCause: Remote storage provider (S3) is throttled; not an application bug.
customerMessage: Backups failed because the storage API is unavailable right now; I'm escalating to platform to coordinate retries.
resolutionNotes: Provider resolved the outage; backups reran successfully later.
